workflows.label: Immutable release installation
requirements: Installation switches an immutable current release
One previous release remains available

A double-click Windows installation asks the user to choose one absolute Personal Agent root and keeps immutable Core, mutable Workspace data, installation staging, upgrades, rollback and uninstall state under that selected root; explicit CLI installation remains non-interactive and macOS behavior is unchanged

Fresh Windows and macOS installation opens the local Setup Center in the Tauri desktop shell; fresh Linux installation uses a version-bound one-line GitHub Release installer, ships no desktop shell or WebView dependency, enables a persistent systemd user service, and exposes Setup Center only through loopback for SSH forwarding

The desktop silently starts managed Cloud enrollment, Sites domain allocation and Agent mail identity synchronization; only a failed or cancelled silent attempt exposes manual recovery and explicit disconnect suppresses automatic reconnection

Sites and local mail expose a keyboard-accessible custom-domain choice beside the platform-domain action; the three-step self-hosted flow clearly requires a separate public Linux server, shows a copyable version-bound GitHub Release installer whose self-extracting asset embeds the Relay and is covered by public checksums and signing evidence, accepts the server-issued Relay key in a password field, binds only the key digest and domain in its approved R2 fingerprint, keeps plaintext only in local Secrets while the server persists only a digest, configures outbound WSS without WireGuard or local administrator rights, maps the apex and Space subdomains across the installation, and commits only after final HTTPS content or real local-mail evidence; without a verified platform or custom domain, Sites and local mail remain warning-state “未生效” connections and are excluded from the effective view even though their local processes and data are preserved

The Windows and macOS desktop shell uses the system WebView and owns the client lifecycle; Linux has no desktop runtime and remains continuously supervised by systemd, with every platform bound to current/previous rollback
The local Console and main Agent share one digest-bound update plan, verified platform artifact, platform-appropriate native handoff, and persisted restart result

A pre-release self-contained candidate can create a ten-minute digest-bound plan only when its commit, manifest, checksums, SBOM, candidate security metadata and platform agree; it reuses the platform-appropriate native handoff and immutable installer, records candidateAssetRuntime=true, and never satisfies public Release acceptance

Applying executable updates normally supports authenticated local approval; a registered Owner-initiated product-development flow authorizes the exact digest-bound plan by policy without a second local confirmation

Managed Cloud and self-hosted reverse tunnels delegate Web and mobile routes to the local gateway except installation-level Space listing and management, which both the tunnel connector and remote gateway reject; mobile remains bound to the current Host's Space, while Space switching and creation require the marked local non-mobile desktop surface; direct loopback access needs no login, tunneled routes on the explicit public whitelist remain public, every other tunneled path requires the host-scoped access session before normal route resolution, and password reset invalidates existing remote sessions
Browser and CLI recovery remain available
Platform releases bundle the pinned OpenCLI production graph without lifecycle scripts or customer-side npm installation, while Chrome retains explicit Browser Bridge permission

workflows.label: Authenticated browser login
requirements: Tunneled login requires explicit local secrets while direct loopback Console access needs no login
Session cookies are host-only and tenant-bound
Changing the local access password invalidates existing remote sessions
Unsafe return locations are rejected

workflows.label: Local Agent conversation
requirements: A local session stores ordered commands and events
Concurrent input is serialized
In-progress Worker tasks resume after a service restart, surface live recovery status, and report completion through the main Agent
A Work reports verified artifact information in its final chat reply while the generic completion event remains domain-neutral, and the main Agent uses that information to author Activity
Child tasks require an Agent-generated title of at most 20 characters and description of at most 100 characters, and both fields remain updateable through the Personal Agent CLI
Child task execution preserves the complete latest visible parent request and all delegated prompt text, including content split by nested shell quotes
Internal worker hook inputs never appear in user conversation history

Mobile task detail reads only the Space-local display-event ledger: it opens on the latest 20 visible events at the absolute bottom, pages earlier events with a task-bound opaque cursor while preserving the reading anchor, follows sanitized live updates only when pinned to the tail, and never scans or falls back to raw Codex execution history
The desktop conversation merges complete desktop and WeChat main-session history chronologically and labels each user message with its source

Desktop and mobile conversation readers preserve structured final-reply image and file attachments with safe same-origin preview/download actions, image dimensions and alt text, file names and sizes, and pending, sent, failed, or ambiguous delivery state

Canonical Task, Page and private-file records keep same-origin relative paths; task CLI results expose a complete Managed Mobile HTTPS url when available or an empty url with linkNotice when unavailable, and no caller concatenates a domain with internalUrl; drive paths, absolute filesystem paths, file URLs and loopback URLs never enter a remote reply
Conversation execution does not require Cloud
Release and final acceptance install the GitHub Release asset and use authenticated /app/chat
A unique prompt reaches the real Agent runtime and its Agent reply is observed in the same session
wechatRequired is false and the optional connections.wechat Setup check never blocks the Agent
Only the canonical main Agent can operate Activity
Activity is never generated from system events
Titles are limited to 30 visible characters and attachments to ten
A good Activity states the outcome, meaning and next action, links a stable governed target when available, and uses only explicit managed attachments
Activity attachments and final-reply attachments remain separate contracts, and a Worker can report artifact objectIds but cannot select or send reply media
Search and update are owner-local and revision-safe

Existing-result retrieval searches Activity before session history, never creates a child task merely to find a result, and falls back across safe local indexes before exposing internal authentication mechanics
The daily Token limit defaults to 0 (unlimited), uses M units, and replies without starting the Agent when today's usage has reached a configured positive limit

Every real main-Agent user turn recalls at most twelve relevant active memories from the current Space before execution, counts only actually injected records once per session-turn-memory tuple, and never gives the capability to Workers

Memory stores content plus system metadata only, supports main-Agent create, revision-safe update and permanent delete through pa-cli, reactivates forgotten records on update, and exposes only authenticated read-only UI routes
Memory heat combines 90-day recency and logarithmic hit frequency, active records are ordered by heat, and records become forgotten after one year without creation, update, or hit

workflows.label: Remote conversation channels
requirements: Personal WeChat is available only on Windows; macOS and Linux omit it from the connection catalog and reject its API, callback, and CLI surfaces

Every connection that persists configuration shows Clear Configuration after setup and returns to Configure only after its endpoint has removed the saved connection state; browser-only read connectors do not claim to own or clear browser account state

DingTalk uses the pinned official Stream SDK for text and media conversations; validates credentials before storing them mode 0600 under the current Space, binds one internal app to one Space, acknowledges and deduplicates callbacks before Agent work, downloads supported image, file, audio, video and rich-text media only from trusted DingTalk hosts into the owning Space and registers it as managed objects, uploads and replies with selected safe images or files only through the source conversation's trusted DingTalk webhook, enforces bounded media sizes, exposes no secret, token, signed media URL or webhook in status, and releases ownership on clear while preserving conversation history and user files
Clearing WeChat claw or personal WeChat releases its installation-level account owner so another isolated Space can configure the identity, while preserving local conversation history
WeChat claw and personal WeChat account identities are each owned by exactly one isolated Space per installation; conflicting Spaces fail closed for status, polling, callbacks and sends

Clearing a configured personal WeChat connection removes its local endpoint, secret, account binding, access policy, connectivity test and pending plans, releases the installation-level account owner, preserves chat history, and restores the Configure action
WeChat identity maps to a reusable local Agent session

WeChat claw generates login QR codes through the official iLink host without inheriting unrelated model egress proxies by default, supports an explicit dedicated or opted-in system proxy, bounds and retries QR requests, validates QR and polling responses, and returns actionable redacted errors
Health failure asks for confirmation before login
Verification codes are bounded and are not persisted as conversation input
Cloud resource binding sends a same-origin browser authorization link without intercepting ordinary conversation or exposing the resource token
WeChat materializes system-relative links on the current managed HTTPS domain and fails closed before any local path or loopback URL can leave the Node

Inbound WeChat files are stored under the owning Space workspace and registered as managed obj_ objects, while the immediate receipt names or counts the files without exposing private preview, batch, local-path, object-store, or loopback links

The independent personal WeChat connector publishes one fixed 127.0.0.1:8843 callback origin, uses the callback root for the personal Space and a trailing user-defined Space code for a child Space, routes that loopback callback to the selected running Space, pins callbacks to the checkWeChat account, rejects expired Pro authorization, never exposes SafeKey, defaults to deny, and only routes policy-authorized recvMsg text callbacks into the main Agent conversation

Personal WeChat reads contacts and groups from Qianxun Pro before accepting a policy, rejects unknown identifiers, deduplicates callbacks, excludes self messages, and sends replies through Qianxun Pro rather than WeChat claw

Personal WeChat persists every normalized recvMsg in a local per-conversation history, exposes opaque conversation and paginated history CLI queries, and supplies the previous at most 100 same-conversation records to each dispatched Agent request as untrusted context

The canonical main Agent may select at most ten current-Space managed obj_ images or safe files in one versioned final-reply envelope; the service strips the envelope, validates ownership, safety, MIME, magic bytes, extensions and size, sends reply text followed by ordered native image/file messages through the same originating WeChat or DingTalk connector, and stores idempotent per-part receipts without exposing paths or silently falling back to bare URLs
Qianxun Pro account writes require a digest-bound R2 or R3 plan, authenticated local human approval, and a semantic allowlisted operation

workflows.label: Task-based automation
requirements: The schedules capability is registered as implemented and the Personal Agent Skill routes reminder requests through pa-cli cron

The desktop exposes scheduled plans only as the task module's Automation subview; /app/automations and /app/schedules redirect to /app/workers/schedules and no standalone automation product or editable rule engine remains
A scheduled task persists its cron expression, IANA timezone, prompt, enabled state and next run, and the Agent verifies mutations by listing persisted schedules
Schedules reject intervals shorter than fifteen minutes
A due schedule creates a normal local task and records its execution without depending on Cloud
Built-in mail scanning may directly create a normal task after protection checks but does not create an automation rule, run, template, or automation Activity

workflows.label: Social browser read connector baseline
requirements: Connections that depend on OpenCLI browser control are available only on Windows and macOS; Linux omits them from the catalog, performs no background probe, and rejects their API and CLI surfaces
Historical account login is coordinated without leaking verification codes
Read-only search results are normalized
Invalid legacy login and QR input is rejected
Current social status inspects only the browser executor, and opening a site creates no account connection
OpenCLI execution is allowlisted, bounded, shell-free, and does not receive unrelated Personal Agent secrets
Twitter/X exposes normalized search and thread reads without inspecting login state or exposing OpenCLI write commands

workflows.label: Pages publication and private preview
requirements: Private publication records remain authenticated
Rendered pages do not expose internal system modules
Published and private content have distinct routes
Internal Page and thumbnail URLs remain relative to the current desktop or mobile origin
pa-cli pages publish returns the managed-tunnel HTTPS URL alongside the canonical internalUrl, or an explicit unavailable-domain notice when no accessible domain exists
Public shareUrl is separate and never replaces the managed delivery URL or internal URL
Every new Page persists different desktop and mobile screenshots and each client renders its matching screenshot
Page publication returns a stable pageId and Page Activity must target that id so the Activity card opens Page detail
Page Activity uses the stored mobile screenshot as representative feed media without scraping Page HTML
The authenticated Pages module exposes a compact static template catalog whose screenshot matches the detail SU style and a detail preview without rendering WebGL in the template list

The built-in renovation delivery template is linked to interior-design, keeps SU design, the user-supplied redacted floor plan, annotated revisions and the Agent requirement digest in one delivery, locates demolition by plan adjacency, uses architectural dimension lines for measurements, keeps label visibility available in mobile landscape, supports desktop and mobile landscape touch layouts, and exposes no browsing-space or desktop wheel hint in the mobile surface

workflows.label: Encrypted backup and restore
requirements: Backup is encrypted and SQLite-aware
Restore verifies data before activation
Scheduled archives and locks are bounded

workflows.label: Previous release rollback
requirements: Rollback atomically swaps current and previous
Inactive release pruning preserves the rollback boundary
Failed or explicitly requested client updates use the retained previous release without downloading another artifact and persist the post-restart result
